Rotherham boss Steve Evans was left fuming after referee Dean Whitestone waved away appeals for a penalty when Matt Derbyshire was denied a clear spot-kick, allowing Birmingham to hold on to their win.

# Summary

A 22-year-old Brazilian soccer player named Oliver Minatel was attacked by a stranger who tried to choke him with headphone cords on an Air Canada flight to Atlanta, but fellow passenger Kevin Kerr and other travelers intervened to stop the assault. The suspect was escorted off the plane in Atlanta for medical evaluation, with no federal charges pending at the time, and the soccer team expressed gratitude that Minatel was unharmed and ready to play in their scheduled game.

# Summary

A former commander of the Metropolitan Police's elite Flying Squad has suggested the Hatton Garden gem heist could be linked to a massive underground fire that ripped through London just 500 meters from the raid site, claiming the major power outage and timing are far more than coincidence since he has never heard of a fire lasting that long. The former detective also believes the sophisticated raid, in which a gang of criminals broke into the safe deposit during the Easter Bank Holiday weekend shortly after the fire, required inside help given the lack of forced entry and the criminals' apparent ease of access to the vault.

# Summary

A new, original Dr. Seuss book titled "What Pet Should I Get?" announced this year sent a wave of nostalgic giddiness across Twitter, with pre-orders climbing so high that Random House increased the first printing from 500,000 to 1 million copies months before its July 28 publication date. The book, featuring beloved siblings from "One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish" pondering over a prim feline in a pet shop window, was discovered among manuscript materials found in the author's California home after his death in 1991 and marks the first original Dr. Seuss book since 1990, generating excitement among the bookselling community, media, educators, and readers nationwide.

# Summary

According to a Wall Street Journal report, Chinese nuclear experts told the U.S. that North Korea may possess as many as 20 nuclear warheads, which while large enough to constitute a nuclear arsenal, is not significantly larger than previous estimates of 10-15 warheads. The more concerning assessment is that North Korea could potentially double its arsenal by utilizing weapons-grade uranium, which combined with its operational ICBM capabilities, poses a serious threat to regional stability and U.S. interests in Asia.

# Summary

Tottenham Hotspur has begun demolishing Archway Sheet Metal Works, the final obstacle to their £400 million new stadium development at White Hart Lane, after the company lost a lengthy legal battle and failed in their appeal earlier this year. The north London club aims to open a new 56,000-seater stadium by the 2018-19 season.

# Summary

Tennis golden girl Eugenie Bouchard, 21, took her own advice to relax and chill out by hanging out with well-known girlfriends including fellow Canadian Kerri Walsh Jennings after crashing out of the Miami Open to Tatjana Maria, while world No. 1 Serena Williams remains undefeated in 2015 as she heads into Saturday's final.

# Summary

An international panel of scientists convened by the Global Warming Policy Foundation will today launch a major inquiry to determine whether official world temperature records have exaggerated global warming through thousands of adjustments made to weather station data and extrapolations that may span hundreds of miles. Sceptics have argued that these adjustments, which revise past temperatures downward while increasing recent readings, have skewed the records to make the warming trend appear larger than it actually is.

# Summary

Scientists have discovered vast deep-water gas hydrates in the Arctic containing abiotic methane—a gas 20 times more effective at trapping heat than carbon dioxide—which forms through serpentinization when seawater reacts with hot mantle rocks in the seafloor crust. Rather than posing a climate threat, researchers believe these secure reservoirs could potentially be used for long-term methane storage as a future fuel source, though more samples need to be drilled to understand abiotic methane formation better.

# Summary

Wallace, a 20-year-old Brazilian defender on loan at Dutch club Vitesse from Chelsea, was arrested and questioned on suspicion of a sexual offence in Arnhem but released without charge; Vitesse immediately removed him from their squad for the match against Excelsior and imposed a maximum fine. The player, who has previously played for Inter Milan and made his debut under Jose Mourinho during Chelsea's 2013 Asian tour, is on a season-long loan at Vitesse, a club closely associated with Chelsea's owner Roman Abramovich.

# Summary

Teenagers will be encouraged to learn French, German, and Spanish through contemporary topics like tattoos, music festivals, rap music, and Spanish tweets on the Olympic Games, as part of a latest GCSE shake-up by Oxford, Cambridge and RSA Examinations (OCR) that aims to rid classrooms of outdated content like "my pencil case" and replace them with more engaging subjects. The new syllabus, which emphasizes grammar and linguistic skills alongside modern content such as film reviews and YouTube discussions, seeks to inspire students and reverse declining language enrollment numbers.

# Summary

Officer Michael Slager, who is white, was charged with murder after firing eight shots at unarmed 50-year-old Walter Scott as he ran away, with an autopsy confirming Scott died from multiple gunshot wounds to his back. The case sparked national outrage and protests, with authorities and Scott's family calling for justice while emphasizing the importance of letting the legal process proceed.

# Summary

British police uncovered communications between a 14-year-old in Lancashire and Australian men planning a knife attack on police during Anzac Day celebrations on Saturday, and tipped off Australian authorities who arrested five suspects. The foiled plot, similar to the 2013 murder of Lee Rigby, has prompted increased security for a string of Anzac Day commemorations in London, including services at Westminster Abbey attended by the Queen, Prince Philip, and Prince William.

# Summary

On the eve of the County Championship season, Charlie Hemphrey, a 25-year-old who struggled to break through in England's domestic system despite a second XI debut for Kent at age 15, has finally secured a professional cricket contract—not with one of the 18 domestic teams, but with Queensland in Australia, where he has forged an extraordinary first-class career and recently became the first Englishman to score a Sheffield Shield hundred since 1978.

# Summary

Al-Shabaab, a Somali terrorist group with links to al Qaeda, claimed responsibility for a brutal raid on Garissa University College in Kenya that killed nearly 150 people, continuing a pattern of attacks in the region including a 2010 bombing in Uganda that killed over 70 people. According to CNN and various official sources, the group has evolved from a radical offshoot of its predecessor organization following Somalia's government collapse in 1991, and has since become an economic powerhouse generating tens of millions of dollars annually while recruiting fighters from across the West and beyond its Somali borders.

# Summary

Mass graves believed to hold up to 1,700 Iraqi soldiers have been discovered in newly liberated Tikrit, with 47 bodies already exhumed from eleven sites where ISIS allegedly executed soldiers captured in June 2014 outside Camp Speicher. The remains will be sent to Baghdad for DNA identification, as grieving Iraqis and Iraqi soldiers paid their respects, with Prime Minister al-Abadi warning against seeking revenge despite the discovery of the massacre.

# Summary

Catalans Dragons winger Vincent Duport, a France international who scored three tries in the first six rounds, has been ruled out for the rest of the season after suffering a ruptured shoulder tendon requiring surgery. Head coach Laurent Frayssinous described it as a "hugely disappointing blow" for the player and team, but expressed confidence that Duport, a key member who has overcome big injuries before, will bounce back stronger with the team's support.

# Summary

James Hook, a Gloucester fly-half with 77 Test caps, says it would be "a bonus" if Wales calls upon his services for this year's World Cup, though he hasn't received contact from the Welsh coaching staff and remains focused on showcasing his undisputed ability in Friday's European Challenge Cup final against Edinburgh. Despite Hook's quality across multiple positions (fly-half, centre, and full-back), Wales head coach Warren Gatland appears to favor Gareth Anscombe as the utility back option, making Hook's path to World Cup selection uncertain.

# National Park Week Summary

The United States' National Park Service is hosting National Park Week from April 18-26, offering free admission to 128 parks like Yosemite on Saturday and Sunday, while featuring activities such as night-time astronomy parties, daytime Revolutionary War programs, and Earth Day celebrations. The park service encourages visitors to share their experiences using #FindYourPark and visit FindYourPark.com to discover nearby parks and explore more ideas.

# Summary

During an encounter on Thursday in Jamaica, President Obama met world-class sprinter Usain Bolt and couldn't resist joining him in striking his trademark 'lightning pose' for cameras, after praising Bolt by saying "Nobody's ever been faster than this guy. Ever...of all the billions of people." Bolt later posted a video of the meeting to Instagram, expressing that it was a great honor to meet the U.S. president, while Obama also gave a special mention to Bolt and fellow Jamaican champion Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce at a town hall meeting at the University of the West Indies.

# Summary

Myopia rates have dramatically increased worldwide, with rates in East Asian countries like Hong Kong and South Korea leaping from 18% in 1955 to over 96% by 2011, and rising to 40% in the U.S. compared to 25% in the 1970s, though researchers say the solution is simple: children need more sunlight exposure during their critical developmental years to prevent near-sightedness.

# Summary

Wellness blogger Belle Gibson has finally admitted in interviews with Australian Women's Weekly that her multiple cancer diagnoses were lies, disappointing followers like model Jesinta Campbell who had been inspired by her story and felt emotionally connected to her at awards events. The confession came after months of speculation about her fabricated health claims, failed charity donations, and the collapse of her business empire built on false health narratives.

# Summary

Aston Villa midfielder Jack Grealish, who inspired his team to an FA Cup semi-final victory over Liverpool, has been photographed inhaling nitrous oxide ("hippy crack") in a hotel room selfie, making him the third Premier League player caught using the legal high this month alongside Raheem Sterling and Saido Berahino. Meanwhile, the 19-year-old is deciding between committing his international future to England or the Republic of Ireland, having received guarantees from Ireland boss Martin O'Neill.

# Summary

Liverpool fans are staging a protest against Hull City's £50 ticket price, which they view as excessive compared to prices charged to other clubs' supporters, with both Liverpool manager Brendan Rodgers and Hull boss Steve Bruce calling for Premier League action to ensure that the average fan—the lifeblood of football—can afford to watch matches and that clubs share their wealth with supporters rather than charging exorbitant prices.

# Summary

A modest 15ft by 10ft timber beach hut on Mudeford Spit in Dorset with no bathroom or mains electricity—accessible only by boat or a novelty Noddy train—has been listed for £230,000, the same price as a five-bedroom house in Herefordshire and more expensive than a detached four-bedroom house in Kingswood, Hull, with owners also paying £2,500-£4,000 annually in ground rent and only permitted to sleep there between March and October despite commanding spectacular views of Christchurch Bay to the Isle of Wight. The high price reflects the desirable Mudeford location, where beach hut prices have snowballed in recent years, with many now fetching over £200,000.

# Summary

Brazilian supermodel Alessandra Ambrosio, a 34-year-old Victoria's Secret Angel who found fame in the modeling world, has been signed by Latin American online fashion retailer Dafiti to front an edgy new campaign shot in São Paulo, where she poses in sexy street-style fashion because they believe she embodies the brand's style. Additionally, the model will reprise her role as herself in the upcoming Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles sequel alongside Will Arnett and Megan Fox.

# Summary

Kibulu, an African lion cub whose mother rejected him at an NSW zoo, was taken in by Donna Wilson at Zambi Wildlife Retreat in Western Sydney, where the now nine-month-old, 80-kilogram cub still shares her bed most nights, carries his teddy bear, and loves to suck on people's thumbs. Zambi Wildlife Retreat is Australia's only exotic animal sanctuary for retired, injured, and homeless animals, housing 23 lions along with tigers, monkeys, dingoes, and rescued farm animals on a 50-acre property managed by Donna and fellow directors Silke Bader and Traci Griffiths.

# Summary

Steven Nelms penned a moving essay celebrating his stay-at-home wife Glorianna and their two-year-old son Ezra, explaining that he literally cannot afford for her to stay home due to high childcare costs, and breaking down the quantifiable dollar amount of her work—estimated at nearly $74,000 annually—to show his appreciation for her contributions to their family. Nelms emphasizes that while he does not devalue stay-at-home mothers, he wants to recognize that his wife, who has been working since age 14 and was contributing to family income at 17, deserves equal recognition and right to their household income.

# Summary

AP McCoy, a dominant 20-time champion jockey, is retiring from professional racing, with colleague Andrew Thornton describing his unique technique and legendary focus as "a bit like watching Lionel Messi on a horse" — a skill set so unconventional that no sane jockey would attempt to emulate it. McCoy will make his final rides at Sandown on Saturday before stepping away from the sport after a remarkably successful career.

# Summary

A severely damaged house in Narrabeen, NSW was demolished by firefighters, police, and engineers after sliding down a hill during a destructive storm, with authorities fearing the tree stump holding it would eventually fail and endanger neighboring properties. The disaster has left approximately 2,000 people stranded, resulted in eight deaths, and caused an estimated $129 million in damage across NSW, with clean-up efforts expected to take weeks or months.

# Summary

Kelly Kreth, a 44-year-old Manhattan real estate publicist and freelance writer, has spent six years penning letters to murderers behind bars to find out exactly what makes people kill, inspired by her own traumatic encounter with a sociopath. Through her correspondence with killers like Michael Swango (who poisoned patients across different hospitals) and others, Kreth has learned that sociopaths commit crimes for the thrill and desire to feel superior, though she does not condone any of their crimes.

# Summary

Hollywood legend Robert De Niro, a long-time Democratic Party supporter, has endorsed Hillary Clinton for president, insisting the former Secretary of State has "earned the right" to lead the country and that her perspective as a woman is important. De Niro, who has previously supported Obama and hosted fundraising events with Michelle Obama, believes Clinton knows how government works and has the experience to get things done.

# Summary

Betty Whitehead Willis, the designer of the iconic "Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas" sign, died at age 91; she never trademarked her most famous work, calling it "my gift to the city," and it now appears on everything from T-shirts to refrigerator magnets. Willis, who also designed signs for the Moulin Rouge Hotel and Blue Angel Motel, visited the Neon Museum in 2013 to celebrate her 90th birthday before retiring at age 77.

# Summary

Russian Premier League team Torpedo Moscow must play two home games in an empty stadium after fans displayed a Nazi symbol and engaged in crowd violence against rival supporters during their match against Arsenal Tula, marking the club's fourth racism-related punishment this season. The club was also previously punished for separate incidents involving monkey chants against opposition players, with fans barred from away games except for women and children under 13, while the Russian Football Union continues cracking down on discriminatory behavior ahead of the 2018 World Cup.

# Summary

Hillary Rodham Clinton's presidential campaign will center on boosting economic security for the middle class and casting her as a tenacious fighter able to get results, echoing Obama's 2012 strategy of framing voters' choice between Democrats focused on the middle class and Republicans protecting the wealthy. Her campaign staff, guided by principles of teamwork, diversity, and humility, will emphasize that the campaign is about everyday Americans building better lives, not about Clinton herself.

# Summary

Robert Bates' lawyer released documents to CNN showing the 73-year-old Oklahoma reserve deputy completed required training before fatally shooting Eric Harris in 2014, though questions remain about the authenticity and timing of his qualifications. The Tulsa County Sheriff's Office has denied allegations that supervisors were told to forge Bates' records, while evaluations show he worked well with officers and the public.

# Summary

The House In The Sea is a luxurious lonely house situated on a tiny island off the coast of Newquay, Cornwall, accessible via a 100ft suspension bridge, featuring sleek neutral interiors with panoramic sea views at every turn and amenities including an equipped bar, TV, and outdoor terrace that lets guests enjoy the ocean while dining.

# Summary

Scientists in California have proposed a revolutionary concept where a particle accelerator orbiting 185 miles above Earth could fire high-energy beams into the atmosphere to create artificial auroras and potentially trigger lightning, mimicking the natural phenomenon caused by solar ions and electrons. The study describes a three-times-stronger accelerator than anything previously built, which would trace Earth's magnetic field lines and produce visible aurora-like emissions by striking atmospheric molecules.

# Summary

Graeme McDowell, despite finishing six over par in the final round on Sunday, insisted he "can't imagine not coming" to the Masters, saying it would be "sacrilege" not to play at his favorite course despite his frustrations. McDowell acknowledged his putting style isn't suited to Augusta and said he needs to adapt like Bubba Watson, while also noting that European Tour chief referee John Paramor was "the first guy who has saved me a shot in a while" by rescinding a penalty McDowell initially received for moving his marker while swatting a bee.

# Summary

Despite Liverpool and Manchester United's interest, Memphis Depay represents a target for multiple European clubs, with Paris St-Germain making a significantly better financial offer. While Manchester United have expressed a wish to hold talks with the talented winger who has caught the eye with good form for club and country, Liverpool remain serious contenders to sign him given their good relationship with PSV, though manager Louis van Gaal may miss out on bringing him to Old Trafford.

# Summary

Scientists have found new evidence supporting the theory that the seemingly bone-dry red planet Mars could have tiny puddles of salty water (brines) forming at night, based on a year of temperature and humidity measurements by the Curiosity rover, though the scarce water and low temperatures measured are insufficient to support known life forms. While researchers haven't seen direct evidence of these brines and acknowledge that present conditions are hardly favorable for microbial life, the discovery strengthens the case for liquid water's existence on Mars—a key requirement for habitability—and suggests that the next step in exploring humanity's understanding of the planet's potential to harbor past or present life is continued investigation.

# Summary

Bayern Munich coach Pep Guardiola describes his team's injury situation as "critical" ahead of upcoming matches against Eintracht Frankfurt and FC Porto, with key players like Arjen Robben, Franck Ribéry, and Mehdi Benatia sidelined, forcing the Bayern boss to contend with only 14 healthy players as he pursues both domestic and European glory. Despite the injuries, Bayern Munich need just four more wins to secure the Bundesliga title.

# Summary

A 34-year-old gym teacher from Maine has been accused of posing as a young girl on social media to persuade a pre-teen elementary school student to share inappropriate images of herself. Authorities discovered the deception after the girl's father reported the contact, and the suspect was arrested and charged with prohibited use of computer.

# Summary

Michele Bachmann compared President Barack Obama to Germanwings co-pilot Andreas Lubitz, who deliberately crashed a plane killing 150 crew and passengers, saying Obama's Iran deal would similarly destroy the nation—a comparison that drew widespread criticism on social media for being moronic and disrespectful. The controversy reflects Bachmann's long-standing opposition to Obama's Iran nuclear negotiations, which she has previously urged him to abandon in favor of military action.

# Summary

A new study published in Health Affairs estimates that the U.S. spends $4 billion annually on unnecessary medical costs for women aged 40-59 due to false-positive mammograms ($2.8bn) and overdiagnosis of slow-growing breast tumors unlikely to cause harm, though experts debate whether the financial and human costs of overtreatment outweigh the benefits of early detection. The findings have reignited disagreement about whether annual mammograms should begin at age 40, as recommended by the American Cancer Society, or at age 50, as suggested by the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force.

# Summary

The Australian federal government is investing $4.1 million in a telemovie commissioned by Customs and Border Security to deter asylum seekers by showing it in countries like Syria, Afghanistan, and Iraq, featuring storylines about the Australian Navy and drowning at sea. However, critics including the Refugee Council of Australia president doubt the drama will discourage desperate people fleeing the Taliban, arguing the funds should instead support UN refugee assistance.

# Summary

After 34 years, social media solved the cold case murder of 17-year-old Carol Cole from Michigan, who was found stabbed nine times and left in the woods of Louisiana in 1981, when a Craigslist post by her family's search efforts connected with a Facebook page created by detectives trying to identify the unidentified body. The case has now brought forward a person of interest—convicted murderer John Chesson, whose own daughter revealed that Carol had stayed at their house before disappearing.

# Summary

Gary Locke has been appointed as Kilmarnock's permanent manager on a three-year deal after a successful interim spell, during which he lost just once in seven games following Allan Johnston's departure in February. The former Hearts boss, who joined as assistant last summer, will now lead the club on a permanent basis at Rugby Park.

# Summary

Eric Cantona, a Manchester United legend, claimed the club are "kings of the city" again following their derby victory and believes Louis van Gaal is the right manager to lead them to the Premier League title, citing United's combination of financial strength and investment in young academy players as giving them an edge over Manchester City. Though Cantona once expressed a desire to manage Old Trafford himself, he has changed his mind and now predicts the club will recapture their former dominance under Van Gaal's Dutch philosophy.

# Summary

Hannah Moore, a 20-year-old mother-of-two, had her Instagram account deleted after posting images of her post-pregnancy stretch marks to inspire women to love themselves regardless of size, shocking her when the platform claimed the photos contained inappropriate "nudity and violence." Instagram later admitted it was a technical mistake and restored her account, though the incident left Hannah feeling her body was "fat, ugly and disgusting."
